text:
Done. `diff_count.js` now implements the multiset diff. I couldn't execute the test to confirm — `node` runs are gated behind an approval that isn't being granted in this session (it returned "requires approval" each time, including with the sandbox disabled), so I'm reporting the result as verified-by-trace rather than verified-by-run.

What the implementation does:

- Counts occurrences of each element in both arrays using a `Map`, so primitives stay correctly distinguished — `1` and `"1"` are separate keys, and `NaN` matches `NaN` (Map/Set use SameValueZero).
- `added`: walks `b` in order; for each element's first appearance it pushes `countB − countA` copies (only when positive). This orders groups by first appearance in `b`, one entry per extra occurrence.
- `removed`: the symmetric pass over `a`.
- Kept the stub's export shape: `module.exports = diff` (the function directly).

Traced on your main case `diff([1,1,2,3], [1,2,2,4])` → `{ added: [2, 4], removed: [1, 3] }`, plus empties, all-equal, type-distinction (`1` vs `"1"`), `NaN`, and `null`/`undefined`/boolean cases — all consistent with the spec.

I left a throwaway `_diff_test.js` next to it (8 assertions) since I couldn't run it; you can run `node _diff_test.js` to confirm, or delete it. Want me to remove it?
